topic.what: Multi-agent LLM framework for robust Android malware detection combining semantic reasoning with deterministic static analysis
topic.how: Uses ReAct-based multi-agent architecture with macro-level heuristic screening (Reconnaissance Agent), micro-level autonomous forensics (Traceability Agent via Soot/FlowDroid), and multi-dimensional evidence fusion (Verdict Agent)
topic.why_matters: Addresses concept drift and prohibitive token costs in LLM-based malware detection without domain-specific fine-tuning

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total end-to-end cost for deep analysis of single complex APK
characteristics.vs_baseline: Significantly lower than direct LLM approaches limited by context windows
characteristics.context: Heterogeneous model strategy using Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B for heavy tasks, Gemini-3-Pro for adjudication

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1-score consistency across malware evolution years 2017-2021
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline models (DroidEvolver) drop to 13.56% F1 by 2021
characteristics.context: Longitudinal evaluation on AndroZoo spanning 5 years

characteristics.vs_baseline: Comparable to CL-Malware (100%) but with higher precision
characteristics.context: Balances recall with 89.29% precision vs CL-Malware's 85.00%

characteristics.what_it_measures: Zero-shot detection accuracy on unseen CIC-AndMal2017
characteristics.vs_baseline: +13.43pp vs CL-Malware (74.45%) which trained on AndroZoo
characteristics.context: Severe spatial distribution shift between training and test domains

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average tokens consumed per APK analysis
characteristics.vs_baseline: Dramatic reduction vs full APK code input approach
characteristics.context: Macro screening reduces search space from 6565 to 7 system APIs per APK on average

requires: LLM with strong code reasoning capabilities (Gemini-3-Pro tier or equivalent)
moderate computational resources for backend engines

novelty: Zero-shot multi-agent architecture bridging LLM semantic reasoning with deterministic static analysis without fine-tuning
ReAct-based autonomous forensics mechanism driving Soot/FlowDroid for code-level evidentiary chains
Heterogeneous model strategy achieving <$0.10 per APK analysis cost
Three-tier architecture (Reconnaissance-Traceability-Verdict) for interpretable malware conviction
open_problems: Heavily packed or encrypted APKs requiring dynamic analysis
Memory forensics integration for stealthy threats
